title: How to automate the email marketing?
excerpt:
Hi everyone, 
Anyone know about how to automate the email marketing?

posts.author: Yoroflow (Yoroflow)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,
Anyone know about how to automate the email marketing?

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Yoroflow

here is a great little video on how the automated email automations work in monday: Integrate your emails | monday.com tutorials - YouTube

That being said monday emails currently don’t support html in their emails. So monday itself is not a great tool for sending drip campaigns to prospective leads, clients etc…

What I’ve done is to sync all my contacts from monday into an email marketing tool : Mailchimp. There are native monday → mailchimp integrations. which can get you started. But I ended up using integromat so as to get different tags/demographic data into mailchimp for better segmentation.

Hopefully that gets you started on the right foot!
Best Regards!!

posts.author: Kelly2E (Kelly)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tim,

would you mind explaining how you used Integromat?

I’m just finding out how the tool works, I need to segment contact into Mailchimp, once a contact in Monday is added (different country, different list in MC).

TIA!

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Kelly2E

See below a screenshot of the integromat scenario (basic version) which watches when a new monday item is added and syncs to mailchimp.
I added tags to enable segmenting in mailchimp, which is what made the scenario in integromat so simple… But if you have different lists, you could create ‘n’ number of mailchimp routes each with a different list selected for the respective country… However I found that managing most contacts in 1 list and segmenting out is much easier to manage.

Screen Shot 2021-11-02 at 5.10.52 PM1105×849 103 KB

Hope that helps!

posts.author: Michaela_Staffova (Michaela)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello guys, Michaela from Integromat here

First of all I’d like to say how happy I am to see that @timlittletech is already using the power of Integromat . That is awesome. Keep up the great work!

Aaaanyway, if you’d like to learn about using monday.com for managing your email marketing activities, you can head over to:

Manage your email marketing on monday.com
which is a super helpful article that also offers some info about monday automations.

If you’re looking to automate your email marketing processes that feature not only monday.com but also other apps and services, Integromat sure is an option to consider.

It, of course, depends on what exactly it is that you’re wanting to automate. You could, let’s say

  • send out welcome/follow-up/reminder emails featuring info from your monday boards (and potentially also update the boards with a note “email sent”)
  • sync the contacts from your emailing list with monday (basically the reverse of what @timlittletech is doing)
  • getting info about the people who don’t go through with the purchase in your online store, send abandoned shopping cart emails and create matching records in monday to keep track of these potential customers
  • add new customers from your online store to your emailing list as welll as to your monday board

And so many many more. You name it, really

To see what some of the options and possibilities are when it comes to automating your email marketing processes on Integromat, you can check out the list of marketing-related apps that are available on the platform.

Well and to learn about allthe fun stuff you can do with Integromat’s monday integration, you can check out its currently available features (ie. triggers, actions and pre-made templates).

Screenshot 2021-11-05 at 15.25.20813×1013 168 KB

If you like what you see and are thinking about trying Integromat out for yourself, you can simply sign up for a free account and get absorbed by the magic of automation

posts.author: andrus (Andrus Purde)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yoroflow:

Anyone know about how to automate the email marketing?

That’s rather general question and the only correct answer is “it depends”

There are some good replies here already but if anyone wants a good answer to this question I’d suggest to ask a more detailed question. Is it ourbound email / lead generation that needs to be automated or onboarding / lead nurturing? How much is known about contacts, what are critical triggers and touch points and what’s the objective? Apart from Monday, what tools are you using or looking to use?

I’ve done email marketing for 10+ years and even written an email marketing course. Happy to help!
